topic: Absolute Value Inequalities and Their Applications

questions.question: A hiker wants to be within $4$ miles of the campsite. If $x$ represents the distance from the hiker to the campsite, the inequality that represents this situation is

The population of a certain animal species is given by $|x - 10| leq 5$, where $x$ is the number of years since the species was first introduced. What is the range of years when the population is within $5$ units of $10$?
A car's fuel consumption is given by $|x - 25| geq 5$, where $x$ is the speed in miles per hour. What speeds will result in fuel consumption outside the range of $5$ units from $25$?
The absolute value inequality $|x - 4| > 2$ can be written in interval notation as
A park ranger wants to ensure that the temperature is within $10^circ$F of $50^circ$F. If $x$ represents the temperature, the inequality that represents this situation is

A certain species of bird migrates to areas where the temperature is between $40^circ$F and $80^circ$F. If $x$ represents the temperature in degrees Fahrenheit, write an absolute value inequality that represents the temperatures that the birds will migrate to, and graph the solution set on a number line.

A car's fuel efficiency is given by $|x - 30| leq 5$, where $x$ is the speed in miles per hour. What speeds will result in fuel efficiency within $5$ units of $30$ miles per gallon? Write your answer in interval notation and graph the solution set on a number line.

teacher_rules: Remind students to consider both the positive and negative cases when solving absolute value inequalities.
Emphasize the importance of graphing the solution set on a number line.
Encourage students to use real-world examples to illustrate the applications of absolute value inequalities.
